Uses of Interface
com.github.cowwoc.requirements.java.ArrayValidator

Packages that use ArrayValidator
Package
Description
com.github.cowwoc.requirements
The Requirements, DefaultRequirements endpoints.
com.github.cowwoc.requirements.java
Classes used to verify preconditions, postconditions and invariants of core Java types.
com.github.cowwoc.requirements.java.extension
Interfaces that may be extended but not implemented (because the methods contain a generic return type).
com.github.cowwoc.requirements.java.internal
Design guide
com.github.cowwoc.requirements.java.internal.extension
 

  • Uses of ArrayValidator in com.github.cowwoc.requirements

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    static ArrayValidator<boolean[],Boolean>
    DefaultRequirements.validateThat(boolean[] actual, String name)
    Validates the requirements of a primitive boolean array.

    DefaultRequirements.validateThat(byte[] actual, String name)
    Validates the requirements of a primitive byte array.

    Validates the requirements of a primitive char array.

    DefaultRequirements.validateThat(double[] actual, String name)
    Validates the requirements of a primitive double array.

    DefaultRequirements.validateThat(float[] actual, String name)
    Validates the requirements of a primitive float array.

    DefaultRequirements.validateThat(int[] actual, String name)
    Validates the requirements of a primitive int array.

    DefaultRequirements.validateThat(long[] actual, String name)
    Validates the requirements of a primitive long array.
    static ArrayValidator<short[],Short>
    DefaultRequirements.validateThat(short[] actual, String name)
    Validates the requirements of a primitive short array.
    static <E> ArrayValidator<E[],E>
    DefaultRequirements.validateThat(E[] actual, String name)
    Validates the requirements of an Object array.

    Methods in com.github.cowwoc.requirements.java that return ArrayValidator
    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    ArrayValidator<A,E>
    ArrayValidator.asCollection(Consumer<CollectionValidator<Collection<E>,E>> consumer)
    Validates nested requirements.
    ArrayValidator<A,E>
    ArrayValidator.asList(Consumer<ListValidator<List<E>,E>> consumer)
    Validates nested requirements.
    ArrayValidator<A,E>
    ArrayValidator.contains(E expected)
    Ensures that the actual value contains an element.
    ArrayValidator<A,E>
    ArrayValidator.contains(E expected, String name)
    Ensures that the actual value contains an element.
    ArrayValidator<A,E>
    ArrayValidator.containsAll(Collection<E> expected)
    Ensures that the actual value contains all the specified elements.
    ArrayValidator<A,E>
    ArrayValidator.containsAll(Collection<E> expected, String name)
    Ensures that the actual value contains all the specified elements.
    ArrayValidator<A,E>
    ArrayValidator.containsAny(Collection<E> expected)
    Ensures that the actual value contains any of the specified elements.
    ArrayValidator<A,E>
    ArrayValidator.containsAny(Collection<E> expected, String name)
    Ensures that the actual value contains any of the specified elements.
    ArrayValidator<A,E>
    ArrayValidator.containsExactly(Collection<E> expected)
    Ensures that the actual value contains the specified elements; nothing less, nothing more.
    ArrayValidator<A,E>
    ArrayValidator.containsExactly(Collection<E> expected, String name)
    Ensures that the actual value contains the specified elements; nothing less, nothing more.
    ArrayValidator<A,E>
    ArrayValidator.doesNotContain(E element)
    Ensures that the actual value does not contain an element.
    ArrayValidator<A,E>
    ArrayValidator.doesNotContain(E element, String name)
    Ensures that the actual value does not contain an element.
    ArrayValidator<A,E>
    ArrayValidator.doesNotContainAll(Collection<E> elements)
    Ensures that the actual value does not contain all the specified elements.
    ArrayValidator<A,E>
    ArrayValidator.doesNotContainAll(Collection<E> elements, String name)
    Ensures that the actual value does not contain all of specified elements.
    ArrayValidator<A,E>
    ArrayValidator.doesNotContainAny(Collection<E> elements)
    Ensures that the actual value does not contain any of the specified elements.
    ArrayValidator<A,E>
    ArrayValidator.doesNotContainAny(Collection<E> elements, String name)
    Ensures that the actual value does not contain any of the specified elements.
    ArrayValidator<A,E>
    ArrayValidator.doesNotContainDuplicates()
    Ensures that the actual value does not contain any duplicate elements.
    ArrayValidator<A,E>
    ArrayValidator.doesNotContainExactly(Collection<E> other)
    Ensures that the actual value does not contain exactly the specified elements; nothing less, nothing more.
    ArrayValidator<A,E>
    ArrayValidator.doesNotContainExactly(Collection<E> other, String name)
    Ensures that the actual value does not contain exactly the specified elements; nothing less, nothing more.
    ArrayValidator<A,E>
    ArrayValidator.isEmpty()
    Ensures that the actual value is empty.
    ArrayValidator<A,E>
    ArrayValidator.isNotEmpty()
    Ensures that the actual value is not empty.
    ArrayValidator<A,E>
    ArrayValidator.isSorted(Comparator<E> comparator)
    Ensures that the actual value is sorted.
    ArrayValidator<A,E>
    ArrayValidator.length(Consumer<SizeValidator> consumer)
    Validates nested requirements.
